symptoms.symptom_name: Video-observed pallor and early mottling of the hand/forearm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor/mottling is a video-observable proxy for critically reduced arterial perfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive paraesthesia to numbness with new motor weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sensory-then-motor loss signals advancing ischaemia and a narrowing salvage window.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known atrial fibrillation with 3 missed days of apixaban

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Identifies the probable cardioembolic source given sub-therapeutic anticoagulation from missed doses.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perishing cold sensation with a demarcation line

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A temperature demarcation line historically correlates with the level of arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete absence of palpable radial, ulnar, and brachial pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent pulses confirm complete arterial occlusion; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt cessation of flow in the brachial artery on arterial duplex/CT angiography

investigation_details.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound or CT angiography
investigation_details.expected_result: Abrupt cessation of flow in the brachial artery consistent with embolic occlusion
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Level of occlusion unconfirmed; telehealth cannot obtain this and should recommend it occur as part of emergency vascular surgery assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms level and nature of arterial occlusion, guiding embolectomy/thrombolysis planning.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Pallor, progressive paraesthesia/weakness, and perishing cold with a demarcation line reproduce the classic '6 Ps' constellation (minus directly-examined pulselessness and pain, both otherwise present) and mandate emergency ambulance dispatch to a vascular surgery centre.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Arterial duplex ultrasound or CT angiography
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the level and extent of arterial occlusion to guide emergency embolectomy/thrombolysis; cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG and echocardiogram (to confirm AF and screen for intracardiac thrombus)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the underlying cardioembolic source and guides ongoing anticoagulation planning.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient vascular studies as first step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay time-critical embolectomy/thrombolysis within the narrow limb-salvage window.
